title: Phase 4f: Headless bot / scripting client
description:
A scriptable nie-bot binary and/or library for automation, bridges, and testing. No UI. Receives and sends messages programmatically.

## Two integration modes

### Mode A: daemon API client (recommended for most bots)
- Bot talks to a running nie-daemon (nie-0tvc) over its local JSON-RPC HTTP+WebSocket API
- nie-bot is a thin CLI wrapper: reads commands from stdin or a config file, posts to daemon
- Bot author writes logic in any language — they just call the daemon API
- Example: pipe incoming messages to a webhook, auto-respond to payment requests

### Mode B: direct nie-core embedding (for high-performance or offline bots)
- nie-bot links nie-core directly (same as nie-cli today)
- No daemon process required
- Suitable for testing harnesses and relay load generators

## Features
- Config: TOML file or env vars (relay URL, identity keyfile path, log level)
- Event loop: connects to relay, emits JSON events to stdout (one JSON object per line, NDJSON)
- Command interface: reads JSON commands from stdin or a named pipe
- Scripting hooks: optional -- execute a shell command on each message event (with shlex safety from CLAUDE.md)
- Payment automation: respond to PaymentAction::Request with Address automatically (payee bot)
- Built-in test mode: --self-test sends a message to self and verifies delivery (useful in CI)

## Use cases
- Notification bridge: forward nie messages to email/Signal/Slack webhook
- Payment bot: accept automated micropayments for a service
- Load tester: spam the relay with N concurrent connections for stress testing
- CI smoke test: two bot instances exchange a message end-to-end without human interaction

## Dependencies
- nie-0tvc (JSON daemon) — Mode A uses the daemon API; daemon must be designed with bot clients in mind
- nie-hlns (JSON-RPC protocol) — Mode B implements the relay protocol directly
